description: ADM-OSC is an industry initiative for standardization of Object-Based Audio (OBA) positioning data in live production ecosystems, implementing the Audio Definition Model (ADM) over Open Sound Control (OSC).

transport.note: Ports should be user-editable. Port 4001 is recommended for one-way communication. Port 4002 is recommended for return/query messages.

addressing.object_number_note: Object numbers are positive integers, starting from 1. The maximum number of objects is implementation-dependent.
addressing.wildcard_support: OSC wildcards (* and ?) are supported in addresses. For example, /adm/obj/*/gain 0.5 sets gain on all objects.
addressing.bundle_support: OSC bundles can be used to group multiple messages with a common timestamp for synchronicity.

obj.description: An audio object (source) in 3D space. The most commonly used type.

lis.description: The listener position and orientation, used for binaural rendering and 6DOF (Six Degrees of Freedom) applications.

env.description: Global scene or environment messages, not specific to an individual object.

queries.description: To GET the current state of a parameter, send the OSC message without any arguments. The receiver replies with the current value(s) to the sender's IP on the return port.

polar.description: Spherical coordinates centered on the listening position.

axes.azimuth: Horizontal angle. 0° = straight ahead. Positive = left. Range: -180° to 180°.
axes.elevation: Vertical angle. 0° = horizontal. Positive = up. Range: -90° to 90°.
axes.distance: Radius from origin. Normalized 0.0–1.0. Default 1.0 = on the reference sphere.

cartesian.description: Normalized Cartesian coordinates. All values are dimensionless in range -1.0 to 1.0.

object.description: Messages targeting a specific audio object. Replace {n} with the object number (positive integer, starting from 1).

polar_position.description: Azimuth angle of the sound object. 0° = front, positive = left, -90° = right, ±180° = back.
Elevation angle of the sound object. 0° = horizontal, +90° = directly above.
Distance from the origin (listening position). 0.0 = at origin, 1.0 = on the reference sphere. Note: in most renderers, distance does not affect object gain directly.
Packed polar position: azimuth, elevation, distance. Preferred over individual messages for synchronized position updates.

cartesian_position.description: Cartesian X position (left/right). -1.0 = full left, +1.0 = full right.
Cartesian Y position (back/front). -1.0 = full back, +1.0 = full front.
Cartesian Z position (bottom/top). -1.0 = full bottom, +1.0 = full top.
Packed 2D Cartesian position: x and y. For horizontal-plane-only control.
Packed 3D Cartesian position: x, y, z. Preferred over individual messages for synchronized position updates.

extent.description: Horizontal extent (width) of the sound object. 0.0 = point source, 1.0 = maximum width.

gain_and_level.description: Linear gain applied to the audio in the object. 1.0 = unity gain (0 dB). No maximum is specified; receiver should clamp to its capabilities. Conversion: dB = 20 * log10(gain). Example: 0.707 ≈ -3 dB, 0.0 = silence.

Reference distance for physics-based rendering. The normalized distance below which rendering is dimensionless (gain is constant regardless of distance). When dref=1.0, the entire space is the dimensionless reference volume (ADM standard behavior). When dref=0.0, physics-based attenuation applies throughout.

Maximum physical distance in meters corresponding to the normalized distance value of 1.0. Maps the normalized distance coordinate to real-world meters. Also corresponds to the ADM absoluteDistance parameter.
Mute state of the object. 1 = muted, 0 = unmuted (active).

metadata.description: Human-readable name for the audio object. Maximum 128 characters.

listener.description: Messages for listener position and orientation, used in binaural/6DOF rendering. The listener has no object number.

position.description: Listener 3D position in normalized Cartesian coordinates.

orientation.description: Listener head orientation: yaw (left/right rotation around Z axis), pitch (up/down rotation around X axis), roll (tilt around Y axis). Positive yaw = left, positive pitch = up.

environment.description: Global scene or environment messages, not specific to any individual object.

scene.description: Program change or scene change event. Signals a change in the global scene or environment.

sender_client.description: An Object Editor or tracking system sending positioning data to one or more receivers.
notes: Cartesian position data is always normalized (-1.0 to 1.0).
Recommended transmit rate: up to 50 Hz for position data (one message per 20 ms).
Use packed messages (aed, xyz) for atomic position updates.

receiver_server.description: A DAW, ADM renderer, object editor, or bridge that receives and processes ADM-OSC messages.
notes: Handles optional local scaling of normalized data to physical units.
Should clamp out-of-range values rather than fail.
Should reply to queries (messages without arguments) with current values.
Examples: DAW (Nuendo, Ovation), renderers (L-ISA, SPAT Revolution, d&b Soundscape).

minimum_viable_sender: Implement /adm/obj/{n}/xyz or /adm/obj/{n}/aed for object position (at least one coordinate system)
minimum_viable_receiver: Respond to queries (messages without arguments) by replying to sender
Clamp out-of-range values to valid range
recommended_additions: OSC wildcard support: /adm/obj/*/gain to address all objects at once

rate_and_timing.recommended_max_rate: 50 Hz (one message per 20 ms) for position data
rate_and_timing.note: Sampling is optional if a parameter has not changed. Do not overload receivers; high-rate position data should use packed messages (xyz/aed) to minimize UDP overhead.
rate_and_timing.interpolation: Interpolation between position updates is not defined in ADM-OSC 1.0 and is left to the implementation.
